intended_use: Rapid Aortic Measurements (AM) is an image analysis and measurement device to evaluate aortic and iliac arteries in contrast enhanced and non-contrast CT imaging datasets acquired of the chest, abdome
intended_use_full: Rapid Aortic Measurements (AM) is an image analysis and measurement device to evaluate aortic and iliac arteries in contrast enhanced and non-contrast CT imaging datasets acquired of the chest, abdomen, and/or pelvis. The module segments the aorta, iliacs, and major branching vessels and provides 2D and 3D visualizations of the segmented vessels.
target_condition: Aortic and iliac artery evaluation in patients with chest, abdomen, and/or pelvis CT imaging
ground_truth: Consensus of up to three clinical experts against source DICOM images for qualitative assessment; approved ground truth segmentations validated with quantitative comparison for measurement accuracy
other_metrics: Segmentation quality (VR outputs): 100% agreement on clinical accuracy; CPR/MPR quality: 100% agreement; Anatomical labeling: 100% agreement; Maximum oblique diameter location accuracy: 100% agreement; Segmentation accuracy (VR outputs): average Dice coefficient 0.93, average Hausdorff distance 0.54 mm; Centerline accuracy (CPR/MPR): average Hausdorff distance 0.59 mm; Ground truth reproducibility: average Dice coefficient 0.95; Measurement accuracy: mean absolute error (MAE) 0.22 cm
